items.title: U.S. Amasses Firepower Near Venezuela: Here’s What It Means for Trump and Maduro | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content: <article>The Pentagon is moving some of its most advanced units and weapons closer to Venezuela as tensions run high between President Trump and Nicolás Maduro.  WSJ’s Shelby Holliday maps the buildup in the Caribbean.</article>

items.title: This Field Disappears In a Matter of Minutes
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content: <article>We went inside Tottenham Hotspur Stadium to see the hidden engineering that helps maximize revenue.</article>

items.title: The NFL’s Secret Weapon in London Is This Hidden Stadium Tech | WSJ Tech Behind
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Ahead of the Denver Broncos vs. New York Jets NFL international game in London, WSJ went inside Tottenham Hotspur Stadium to see the hidden engineering that helps maximize revenue by transforming this English football pitch into a stage for world tours, a boxing ring and even an American football field.

0:00 This English Premier League field vanishes in minutes
0:33 The challenge
2:11 The solution
7:38 The payoff

Tech Behind 'The Tech Behind' explores the amazing engineering, computing, science and algorithms that power our favorite tech.

#Sports #Tottenham #WSJ</article>

items.title: China, North Korea and Russia: A Complicated Geopolitical Love Triangle
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content: <article>As China’s Premier Li Qiang visits North Korea for the ruling party’s 80th anniversary, Beijing may remind Pyongyang who its real ally is. WSJ’s Lingling Wei explains. Photo: Alexander Kazakov/Zuma Press</article>

items.content:
<article>Investors are snapping up gold, pushing the commodity above $4,000 a troy ounce for the first time. WSJ’s David Uberti uses three charts to help explain what’s behind the rush and what could come next.

items.title: Government Shutdown: When Will the Military Troops Get Paid? | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>As the government shutdown enters its second week, more than 1.3 million active-duty U.S. military service members are at risk of missing a paycheck, if a deal isn’t reached soon. WSJ’s Shelby Holliday explains what happens now.

#wsj #thewallstreetjournal #shutdown #usmilitary #nationalguard</article>

items.title: Shutdown Day 8: Air Travel Troubles Ahead as Shutdown Drags On
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content: <article>WSJ’s Jacob Passy breaks down how the ongoing U.S. government shutdown is impacting air travel, with thousands of flights delayed due to staffing shortages.</article>

items.title: How Much Longer Can Grocery Stores Delay Trump’s Tariff Price Hikes? | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Grocery prices are on the rise, and early evidence suggests President Trump’s tariffs aren’t helping. Pineapples from Costa Rica, fish from Greece and cheese from Italy are all subject to Trump’s tariffs, but that doesn’t mean customers are seeing the price increases–yet. 

WSJ visited the grocery store chain Stew Leonard’s and spoke with the CEO to see how tariffs are affecting a handful of popular items. 

Chapters:
0:00 Tariffs and grocery prices
0:35 Inside Stew Leonard’s 
1:08 Grocery inflation
2:11 Shrimp prices
3:02 Produce prices
3:41 Aluminum prices
4:35 What’s next?

News Explainers
Some days the high-speed news cycle can bring more questions than answers. WSJ’s news explainers break down the day's biggest stories into bite-size pieces to help you make sense of the news.

#Tariffs #Inflation #WSJ</article>

items.title: Trump vs Denmark: Inside the Military Effort to Protect Greenland | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Greenland is a self-governing territory of the Kingdom of Denmark, a NATO member that controls the island’s security policy. Its position as a natural barrier between Russia and North America makes it important for the U.S. and President Trump, which has only one active base on the island.

The Danish Armed Forces take WSJ inside its operation as it sets out to prove to the U.S. and Russia that it is capable of defending Greenland. We gained rare access to Denmark’s operations in the vast Arctic landscape: in the air, on land and at sea. 

Chapters:
0:00 Inside a military helicopter in Greenland
0:48 NATO’s frontline in the sea
2:24 Land forces 
4:55 Trump’s pledge to take Greenland
6:03 Remote logistics hub

#Greenland #Denmark #WSJ</article>

items.content:
<article>WSJ Economics Reporter Konrad Putzier explains how the 2025 government shutdown is adding more uncertainty to an already fragile U.S. economy.

#Shutdown #Economy #Shorts</article>

items.title: How Russia Brazenly Tests NATO’s Air Defenses | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Russian drones, jet fighters and other aircraft have increasingly intruded into NATO airspace. In recent months, Russian drones flew deep into Poland, warplanes violated Estonia’s airspace for 12 minutes and Denmark spotted unidentified drones over its main air force base. President Trump has said that European members of NATO should respond with force.

WSJ analyzes how Russia is pushing NATO’s boundaries and the bloc’s strategic options to respond. 

Chapters:
0:00 Russia pushing NATO’s boundaries
0:28 Timeline of events 
2:19 NATO’s response 
4:17 What’s next?

#Russia #NATO #WSJ</article>
